reason: The text contains dialogue from the servants of Pharaoh (עַבְדֵ֨י פַרְעֹ֜ה), who are regular people, not elite. Specifically, at one point the servants of Pharaoh directly address Pharaoh, asking 'How long will this be a snare for us? Send the men away and let them serve the Lord their God' (וַיֹּאמְרוּ עַבְדֵ֨י פַרְעֹ֜ה אֵלָ֗יו עַד־ מָתַי֙ יִהְיֶ֥ה זֶ֛ה לָ֖נוּ לְמוֹקֵ֔שׁ). While these servants are not individually named, they do have direct dialogue, which contradicts the statement that only elite figures have lines.
